pages.raw_text:
Two pieces of official paper,
hanging side by side on the bulle-
tin board in the Office of the county
auditor, form a sharp contrast in
the mind Of the ordinary individ-
ual. One paper sets forth in de-
tail. by lots, the valuation fixed
by the state land department on
property wanted by the Spokane
International railway. The other
paper sets forth in detail, lot by
lot, the valuation fixed by this
same state land department on
property which is offered for sale
in open competitive market to the
citizens of this vicinity. Both pa
pers refer to land the school
section in the eastern part of the
city.


No one doubts but that Spokane
is to be the gamer by the con-
struction and operation of the Cor
bin road, but that is no reason why
the state should make a present of
its land to Mr.. Corbin's road


The contrast between the value
of lots as set forth on the two
pieces of paper Is ridiculous, not
just to the state or to the people
and suggests pnrposefuI valuation
In favor of the railway.


There are So lots wanted by the
railroad, and the valuation fixed by
Mr. Ross office ranges from S. up
toward 800. There is approx
mately an equal number Of lots on
the other list. and few of the valu-
ations for ground alone fall below
5000


To become specific, the lot at
the northeast corner of Cook street
and Broadway, in block 1852, which
is wanted by the railroad, is valued
by the state land Office at $3.50.
